done_rule_playable_mechanic.reason: DONE-RULE requires the playable mechanic to teach 'token-bucket capacity vs refill (admit/reject under a refill budget)'. The 01_rate_limiter slug dispatches encounterKind='sequence_flow' (mechanicName='Agent Quest'), an orchestration duel: the player advances 10 plan-act-observe-verify steps with Z (advance) / X (block guard). No capacity/refill arithmetic, no admit/reject-under-refill decision exists in the playable surface. tokenBucket.ts exists and is registered but is dispatched ONLY for modules without an explicit encounterKind (03_url_shortener, 05_websocket_chat, 10_distributed_cache, 14_log_aggregator, 15_metrics_collector), NOT for 01_rate_limiter. PLAN.md section 3 admits this: 'the token-bucket algorithm is the target under verification, not the direct arcade mechanic'. The connection to 'token bucket' is purely narrative (concept string + briefing text).

notes: Independent fresh-context verdict. All four gate commands + smoke are GREEN; a schema-conformant EVIDENCE record with full review_context IS emitted and the smoke asserting it passes; PLAN slice and screenshot are present and honest. The failure is purely didactic-fit against the ASSIGNED done-rule: the playable mechanic for 01_rate_limiter is sequence_flow (Agent Quest), not token-bucket capacity vs refill. The prior verifier-report.json (scored 9/10 PASS) used a looser done-rule ('sequence-flow Agent Quest emits valid EVIDENCE') and itself admitted 'no actual token-bucket arithmetic is exercised by the player... the connection to token bucket is purely narrative.' Under the stricter assigned done-rule (playable mechanic must teach token-bucket admit/reject under a refill budget), the mechanic is exactly the 'thematic reskin' the grade penalizes, so result=FAIL and score reflects solid engineering but absent didactic fit for the named concept. Evidence is schema-valid (evidence_valid=true) but evidences the wrong scenario, captured under done_rule=FAIL.
